Why Attend Speakers Agenda Venue
Register Now

New York · November 18, 2026

Where payments connect

A full-day summit for the people building the global payments ecosystem — operators, infrastructure leaders, and the decision-makers in between.

One day · New York City · 200+ attendees

Reserve your seat → View agenda
0 Days
0 Hours
0 Minutes

Why attend

The room where payments decisions get made

01

Senior-only access

Attendance is curated and capped. Every conversation happens at decision-maker altitude — no booth staff, no cold pitches.

02

Curated networking

We match you with the ten people you actually need to meet — introductions made before you walk in the door.

03

Real intelligence

Sessions built on operating data and hard-won lessons from people running live payment systems, not vendor decks.

04

Global perspective

Leaders from four continents on rails, regulation, interchange, and where the volume is moving next.

What you'll experience

A day engineered
for signal

Keynotes from industry architects

The people who built the rails take the stage — not to recap the year, but to tell you what they are betting on next. Every keynote is original material, prepared for this room.

Closed-door roundtables

Twenty seats, Chatham House Rule, one hard problem per table. The conversations that never happen on a stage happen here. Pre-registration required; seats are allocated by role.

Evening reception on the NYC skyline

The day closes on a terrace above Midtown. No badges scanned, no sponsored toasts — just the two hundred people in payments you most want an hour with.

Speakers

Voices shaping global payments

Chief Payments Officer

Klarna

Daniel Okafor

Head of Acquiring

Adyen

VP, Open Banking

SVP, Real-Time Payments

Mastercard

Head of Risk & Identity

Stripe

James Calloway

General Manager, North America

Checkout.com

Elena Marchetti

Director, Global Enterprise Payments

Worldpay

Marcus Chen

VP, Network Products

Visa

Agenda

November 18 — Full day

New York City · Doors open 08:30

08:30
Break

Registration & Coffee

09:00
Keynote

Opening Keynote: The State of Global Payments 2026

Where the volume moved, where the margin went, and the three shifts that will define the next five years of the industry.

09:45
Panel

Navigating Real-Time Payments Infrastructure

FedNow, RTP, and instant rails abroad — what adoption actually looks like from inside the institutions running them.

10:30
Break

Coffee Break & Networking

11:00
Roundtable

Roundtable A: Cross-Border Settlement Innovation

Closed door · 20 seats

Roundtable

Roundtable B: Embedded Finance at Scale

Closed door · 20 seats · Runs in parallel

12:00
Keynote

The Merchant Payments Experience Gap

Why the distance between what merchants need and what providers ship keeps widening — and who is closing it.

12:45
Break

Lunch & Curated 1:1 Meetings

14:00
Panel

AI, Fraud, and the Future of Authentication

Risk leaders on synthetic identity, agentic commerce, and what authentication looks like when both sides have models.

14:45
Roundtable

Roundtable C: Issuing & Card Programme Strategy

Closed door · 20 seats

15:30
Break

Afternoon Break

15:45
Keynote

Building Payment Infrastructure That Lasts

A two-decade view on the architecture decisions that age well — and the ones that quietly become the ceiling on your roadmap.

16:30
Fireside

Fireside Chat: Lessons from Scaling a Global Payments Business

An unscripted hour with an operator who took a payments company from one market to forty.

17:15
Closing

Closing Remarks

17:30
Reception

Evening Reception — Skyline Terrace

Past attendees

200+ companies across the payments ecosystem

Visa Mastercard Stripe Adyen PayPal Worldpay Checkout.com Klarna Revolut J.P. Morgan American Express Square Braintree Marqeta Nuvei Paysafe

Venue

Convene, 101 Park Avenue

101 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10178

A full floor of purpose-built conference space two minutes from Grand Central — and a private terrace for the evening reception.

Getting there

Subway

4 · 5 · 6 · 7 and S trains to Grand Central–42 St, two minutes on foot.

Grand Central

Metro-North to Grand Central Terminal; LIRR to Grand Central Madison — both adjacent.

Airports

JFK ~50 min via AirTrain + LIRR. LaGuardia ~30 min by taxi via the Midtown Tunnel.

Parking

Garages on E 40th and E 41st Streets; pre-booking strongly advised on event days.

101 Park Ave
New York, NY

View on Google Maps →

FAQ

Common questions

Senior operators across the payments ecosystem — fintechs, processors, acquirers, issuers, networks, and infrastructure providers. Most attendees hold director-level roles or above; attendance is reviewed to keep the room senior.

Request a seat via the Reserve your seat button. We review requests within two business days and confirm by email with payment and calendar details.

Yes. A full-day pass is $1,250 and covers all sessions, lunch, curated meetings, and the evening reception. Group pricing is available for three or more colleagues.

Yes — each registration may nominate colleagues, subject to the same seniority review. Add their details during registration or email us afterward.

Business casual. The evening reception is on an outdoor terrace — bring a layer for November in New York.

Keynotes and panels are recorded and shared with attendees after the event. Roundtables run under the Chatham House Rule and are never recorded.

Roundtables are capped at twenty seats each and allocated by role to keep every table balanced. Pre-register for your preferred table during registration; confirmations go out two weeks before the event.

Full refund up to 30 days before the event, 50% up to 14 days before. Inside 14 days, passes are non-refundable but fully transferable to a colleague.

Join us in New York
